title: Zillow Group Reports Second-Quarter 2022 Financial Results
text: Zillow Group Q2 2022 Financial Results Summary. Key Performance Metrics: Revenue Performance: Consolidated Q2 revenue reached $1.0 billion, exceeding the midpoint of guidance. Total revenue declined 23% year-over-year from $1.31 billion in Q2 2021. Segment Results: IMT (Internet, Media & Technology): $475 million (flat YoY). Homes: $505 million (down 35% YoY, reflecting iBuying wind-down). Mortgages: $29 million (down 49% YoY). Profitability Highlights: Net Income: $8 million in Q2 2022 versus $10 million in Q2 2021. Adjusted EBITDA: $164 million, surpassing outlook midpoint. IMT segment: $186 million. Homes segment: $(1) million. Mortgages segment: $(21) million. Notable Developments: CEO Rich Barton stated the company "stands on solid ground" with "positive operating cash flow" and a "trusted household brand." The organization announced a partnership with Opendoor to enhance moving services. User Engagement: 234 million average monthly unique users (up 2% YoY); 2.9 billion visits (up 5% YoY). Balance Sheet: $3.5 billion in cash and investments; $1.7 billion in debt.
